# MLB baseline model (Phase 2)

**Model:** Logistic regression on scaled features (see `app/models/mlb_baseline.py`).

**Split:** Train 2023–2024 · Holdout 2025 only (chronological, no shuffle).

**Artifact:** `data/processed/mlb_baseline_model.joblib` (gitignored)

**Metrics:** `data/processed/mlb_baseline_metrics.json` (gitignored; regenerated by train script)

## Holdout metrics (2025)

Train rows: 4,859 (2023–2024) · Holdout rows: 2,430 (2025)

| Model | Log loss | Brier | Accuracy |
|-------|----------|-------|----------|
| home_always_wins | 6.3165 | 0.4572 | 0.543 |
| elo_baseline | 0.6883 | 0.2474 | 0.552 |
| logistic_regression | **0.6777** | **0.2408** | **0.577** |

## Phase gate

**PASS** — logistic regression beats both baselines on 2025 holdout log loss (and Brier / accuracy vs Elo).

Run `python scripts/train_mlb_baseline.py` to regenerate metrics after data updates.

## Imputation (training)

| Field | Rule |
|-------|------|
| ERA | Season median from 2023–2024 training games |
| L10 win % | 0.5 (neutral) when missing |
| L10 run diff | 0.0 when missing |
| Rest days | Median rest days from 2023–2024 training games (fallback: 1) |

FIP is not used (all null in Phase 1 data).
